items.mistake: Applying different encoders ad hoc in notebooks without a ColumnTransformer or Pipeline
items.fix: Define all encoding branches in one ColumnTransformer, wrap with the estimator in Pipeline, and serialize the combined object.
items.why: Ad hoc transforms drift between train and serve; column order and vocabularies become impossible to reproduce.
items.example: Manually calling `pd.get_dummies` in training but forgetting the step in the Flask API—production rows have fewer columns and `predict` crashes.

items.mistake: Choosing one-hot for every string column regardless of cardinality
items.fix: Inspect `nunique()` per column; for high-cardinality IDs use bucketing, frequency, hash, or CV target encoding per Encoding Categoricals guidance.
items.why: Sparse thousands-wide matrices slow training, inflate memory, and encourage memorization of entity IDs.
items.example: One-hot on `customer_id` with 80,000 levels produces a 80k-column matrix on 500k rows—training never finishes on a laptop.

items.mistake: Refitting encoders when new production categories appear
items.fix: Freeze the training artifact; map unseen labels via `handle_unknown`, `__other__`, or zero frequency—retrain on schedule with new data instead of hot-patching encoders.
items.why: Online refit changes feature dimension and statistics without model retraining, breaking consistency and evaluation comparability.
items.example: Nightly job calls `enc.fit` on all historical plus today's rows—dummy columns multiply and yesterday's model weights no longer align.

items.mistake: Encoding raw entity IDs instead of aggregating behavior
items.fix: Drop or replace IDs with count, recency, or target-encoded aggregates computed with proper CV; reserve IDs for join keys, not features.
items.why: Entity IDs encode identity, not generalizable patterns—models memorize individuals and fail on new entities.
items.example: Using `user_id` target-encoded purchase rate without smoothing—each user's own history dominates the feature with no transfer to new users.
